title: Separability from Multipartite Measures
abstract: We show that the third-order negativity provides a necessary and sufficient criterion for full separability of tripartite pure states, and extend this to mixed states beyond bipartite diagnostics such as negativity. As a minimal nontrivial example, a four-qubit pure state has three-qubit mixed reductions; its complete characterization requires six bipartite, eight tripartite, and four quadripartite measures, with the third-order negativity serving as a key separability criterion. We further generalize these separability criteria to multipartite qudit systems and discuss an application to conformal field theory.
summary: This paper establishes new, computable criteria for determining the full separability of multipartite quantum states using third-order negativity. It provides a complete classification of entanglement measures for four-qubit systems and demonstrates how these algebraic invariants can be applied to study entanglement in Conformal Field Theory.
main_problem: The paper addresses the difficulty of classifying multipartite entanglement and establishing necessary and sufficient criteria for full separability in multi-qubit and multi-qudit systems.
main_result: The authors prove that the third-order negativity (I5) is a necessary and sufficient criterion for the full separability of tripartite pure states and develop a complete set of 18 independent entanglement measures for four-qubit pure states.
method: The study employs invariant theory, the replica trick, convex-roof extensions for mixed states, and algebraic decomposition of hyperdeterminants.
model_or_system: The research focuses on multipartite quantum systems, specifically 3-qubit and 4-qubit pure and mixed states, as well as general q-partite qudit systems and applications to 2D Conformal Field Theory.
key_observables: Third-order negativity (I5), three-tangle, two-tangle, four-tangle, and the hyperdeterminant.
important_parameters: Central charge (c) in CFT, number of qubits/qudits (q), and the large-c limit.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the use of local unitary transformations for classification and relies on the compact nature of the parameter simplex for finding extrema.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides graphical notation for tripartite wavefunctions and the construction of the 2nd Renyi multi-entropy.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing separability criteria for tripartite states, moves to the classification of 4-qubit entanglement measures, generalizes these findings to q-partite qudit systems, and concludes with applications to Conformal Field Theory.
why_interesting: This work provides fundamental tools for characterizing multipartite entanglement, which is essential for understanding complex correlations in many-body dynamics and quantum information processing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
